And so now you've got that in. There's usually a footer down at the very bottom. And sometimes it's actually like a bar footer. Sometimes it's just your website and your phone number or something along those lines. You know, it just depends on how you have it designed. But some people don't do footers at all, but usually, there's a footer on a newsletter. Okay, this front page, this is your relationship building article. This is the article where you want to really let people get to know you. So what we do is we, and you can hire this out to other people, I'm sure, do this. But we have our clients, they call in and then they get interviewed by one of our writers and then they ghost write this article for them. So they ask questions and then write the article. So if you're not a big writer, that's absolutely a tip, something you can do. But if you are and you sit down, this is where you're going to talk about stuff that's going on in your life. If you're a business to business, so I always get this question on business to business. This is where you're going to talk about something going on. So if it was my business, I would talk about something that's going on in my life. Maybe one of my kids did something silly and we tied it back around to a business lesson. So I think my article for, so one of my oldest sons just graduated high school last weekend. And so it's going to be the, you know, it's going to talk about that and then we're going to tie it around to our talk about him starting his, you know, his first real business. And so, and kind of some of the business lessons I laid out for him. And so that's going to be my article for next month's newsletter. And so this is where you're going to let people know you open up a little bit here. Now a really cool strategy to increase readership. Readership is everything on the newsletter. I mean, if no one's going to read it, which is what happens on those boring newsletters, it is infinitely more fun to take half the money you would have spent on the newsletter, light it on fire, and then take the other half and go out and have a good time. Because that's basically what you're doing if no one reads your newsletter. So what you want to do is, if at all possible, have the entire first page with your personal article and even if it's just a paragraph, continue on into the inside of the newsletter. And this will, people don't want to like not finish the article. So that encourages them to open it up. And so down at the, so you have your article, you have your headline, you have your article, and then at the very bottom it says continue on page three or whatever. Okay, so now if we go over to page two. So the top of page two, this is where a lot of times people are going to put their featured article. So this is the article that's going to improve the lives of the person who's reading it. So again, if we're going back to our dental example, the 35 to 55 year old mom, this could be the seven tips to keeping your kids safe on Facebook. If we're going to use a B2B example, this could be a work life balance article. I found most business owners struggle with that. And so this is something to improve their lives. It's typically not about your business or even what you do. It doesn't mean that it can't be sometimes, but for the most part, it should really just be focusing on improving their lives. And that's going to take up usually half a page or so. Maybe a little more down at the bottom of page two. So if we have a little, you know, a little line drawn here between page two, the top of page two and the bottom of page two, the second half or the second third, depending on how long that first article is, this would be great for an entertainment section or it could be really good for a local section. So one really popular article we've been creating in the B2C world and even some B2B when all the clients are local is just an article about what's going on in that area. So if you live in Boise, Idaho, you know, we have the music festival coming up here in a month or so. And so we might write about this music festival and Sir Mix-a-Lots is going to be there and we might have a picture of, you know, him and we might even title it like Baby Got Back or I don't know, you know, who knows, right? And in case you're into rap, you would know what I'm talking about there. But so, you know, we'll write about this article, we'll tell them where to go get tickets, we'll tell them how much it's going to cost, you know, tell them when the dates are that they can plan it out. So if it's just something to make it really personal, really local, this could be an entertainment section. So some of these sections are interchangeable. You don't need to have it at the bottom of page two. You can say, hey, I want to have that at the top of page three instead, right? And then you're going to continue going through the newsletter and do the same thing. So if we move over to page three, we're going to talk about, this could be a great spot to talk about our referral promotion that we're running. We could show who won last month's referral contest.
